finding: Agents repeatedly use PowerShell text IO or range-slicing patterns despite repository instructions requiring Node.js UTF-8 helpers for Markdown, JSON, and text planning files.
expectedBehavior: First-layer ATM skills and playbooks should expose copy-paste-safe Node.js UTF-8 read/compare snippets and warn against PowerShell Get-Content/Set-Content/Out-File/range slicing for document work.
evidenceOrRepro: During ATM-GOV-0186/ATM-GOV-0194 dogfood, the operator used PowerShell content commands before noticing the Windows Text Document IO Rule in atm-dispatch/atm-plan-authoring.
followUp: Update source-of-truth ATM skills and integration pack templates so Windows text IO guidance appears in the first visible workflow layer, then reinstall/regenerate derived skill copies.
